title: ConvertKit vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for creators vs ecommerce businesses.
h1: ConvertKit vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for Email Marketers
intro: ConvertKit and Drip are both established email marketing platforms launched in 2013, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit targets creators and course sellers with an accessible free tier, while Drip specializes in ecommerce integration with advanced revenue tracking. Choosing between them depends on your business model and technical needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Ecommerce businesses with deep store integration

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, sequences, landing pages, commerce
comparison_table.tool_b: Visual automation builder, ecommerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller looking for affordable email marketing
You want to start free and upgrade gradually as your audience grows
You value landing pages and built-in commerce features for digital products
choose_b_if: You run an ecommerce store and need deep Shopify or WooCommerce integration
You require advanced revenue attribution and ecommerce tracking capabilities
You need SMS marketing alongside email and don't mind starting at $39/month

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit is better for creators and has a free tier, making it ideal for starting out. Drip is superior for ecommerce businesses needing sophisticated revenue tracking and store integration. Both have identical G2 ratings (4.4), so choice comes down to your specific features and budget needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Drip?
faq.a: ConvertKit is significantly cheaper if you're starting out, thanks to its free tier supporting up to 1,000 subscribers. Once you upgrade, ConvertKit costs $29/month while Drip starts at $39/month. For ecommerce, Drip's pricing may be justified by its specialized features.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email export and import functions. You'll be able to migrate your subscriber list and historical data. However, you'll need to manually recreate automation workflows and sequences, as they don't directly transfer between platforms.
